summary: Emit a loud stderr warning when Surf's underlying *http.Transport is nil in sharedDiaryHTTPClient(), mirroring the existing guard in newHTTPClient.
reason: sharedDiaryHTTPClient previously assigned surfStd.Transport without a nil check. If a future enetx/surf release changes Std()'s shape and returns a client with no Transport, the silent fallback would drop the Chrome TLS fingerprint on the diary scrape path and every request would hit MFP's anti-bot wall with no visible cause. Brings the diary path in line with surf-transport-nil-loud-warning in internal/client/client.go.
